title: helix-cli: Four CLI Ergonomics Improvements
description: This tour walks through four independent CLI improvements made to helix-cli: (1) fixing a field name mismatch in `tickets get` run display (createdAt/completedAt → startedAt/finishedAt), (2) adding a `--dry-run` flag to `tickets continue`, (3) adding a `--search` flag to `tickets list` for server-side filtering, and (4) adding `--query-file` support and corrected PowerShell help examples to `inspect db`. Each change is isolated and follows existing flag-parsing patterns. Two correctness issues were also caught and fixed during code review: a nullable `startedAt` type and broken PowerShell single-quote escaping.

steps.description: Start here to understand WHY all four changes were needed. The diagnosis identified four independent ergonomics gaps: (1) `tickets list` had no server-side search, forcing full fetches and local filtering; (2) `tickets get` showed 'in progress' placeholder text for all run timestamps because the CLI type declared `createdAt`/`completedAt` but the server returns `startedAt`/`finishedAt` — undefined fields silently fell through `formatDate`; (3) `tickets continue` had no preview mode before firing a POST; (4) `inspect db` had no file-based query escape hatch and its PowerShell help examples used single-quoted strings with backtick escapes, which PowerShell 5.1 does not process in single-quoted context.
steps.title: Overview: Four Root Causes Identified

steps.description: The implementation plan establishes the sequencing rationale: S1 (field name fix) is simplest and standalone; S2 (dry-run gate) is a single conditional before an existing API call; S3 (search flag) adds a query param requiring a server-side endpoint; S4 (query-file) adds file I/O with priority logic over --query and positional args; S5 runs quality gates. All four features are independent — the order optimizes for verifying the simplest standalone changes first and deferring server-dependent end-to-end testing to S3. No new runtime dependencies are introduced; only the Node.js built-in `node:fs` is used.
steps.title: Implementation Plan: Five Steps in Logical Order

steps.description: The CLI's `TicketDetail` type (src/tickets/get.ts:14-19) declared run fields as `createdAt: string` and `completedAt: string | null`. The server's `mapRunHistoryItem` (ticket-service.ts:457-473) returns `startedAt` and `finishedAt`. At runtime both fields were `undefined`. The `formatDate` function treated `undefined` as 'in progress' regardless of actual run state, so every run — including SUCCEEDED ones — showed placeholder text. The fix renames both fields in the type declaration and all downstream references.
steps.title: S1 Root Cause: Field Name Mismatch in tickets get

steps.description: Code review caught two issues requiring fixes beyond the original plan. First: `TicketDetail.runs[].startedAt` was typed as `string` but the server returns `null` for QUEUED runs (confirmed by testing `tickets get HLX-65`). The type was corrected to `string | null`. Second: the PowerShell 5.1 help examples in `inspect/index.ts` used single-quoted strings wrapping backtick-escaped content. Per the PowerShell language spec, backtick is only an escape character inside double-quoted strings; single-quoted strings have no escape processing. The outer quotes were corrected from single to double. Both fixes are low-risk and purely additive to correctness.
steps.title: Code Review: Correctness Issues Caught and Fixed

steps.description: In `src/tickets/get.ts`, the `TicketDetail` type's runs array element type is updated: `createdAt: string` → `startedAt: string | null` (null added after code review) and `completedAt: string | null` → `finishedAt: string | null`. All downstream references in `printTicketDetail` that read `run.createdAt` and `run.completedAt` are renamed to match. Data flow: server JSON → `TicketDetail` type → `printTicketDetail` → `formatDate(run.startedAt)` / `formatDate(run.finishedAt, run.status)`. With correct field names, `formatDate` now receives real ISO date strings instead of `undefined`, and SUCCEEDED runs display actual timestamps.
steps.title: S1 Implementation: get.ts Type and Reference Rename

steps.description: In `src/tickets/continue.ts`, `hasFlag` is imported from `src/lib/flags.ts` and a dry-run check is inserted before the POST API call (line 30). If `--dry-run` is present, the resolved continuation payload is printed to stdout and the process exits without making any API call — no side effects. Data flow: args → `hasFlag(args, '--dry-run')` → if true, `console.log(JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2))` + `process.exit(0)`; if false, existing POST call proceeds unchanged. Help text in `src/tickets/index.ts` is updated to document the new flag. This is the minimal correct change: one import, one conditional, one help line.
steps.title: S2 Implementation: --dry-run Gate in tickets continue

steps.description: In `src/tickets/list.ts`, `getFlag` (already imported) is used to parse `--search` from args. If present, `search` is added to the `queryParams` object that is serialized into the GET `/api/tickets` request URL. Data flow: `getFlag(args, '--search')` → `queryParams.search = value` → server receives `?search=<value>` → server's `listTicketsForOrganization` applies `title: { contains: search, mode: 'insensitive' }` Prisma filter (PostgreSQL ILIKE, no migration needed). If `--search` is absent, `queryParams` is unchanged and existing behavior is preserved. Help text in `src/tickets/index.ts` documents the new flag.
steps.title: S3 Implementation: --search Flag in tickets list

steps.description: In `src/inspect/index.ts`, `readFileSync` is imported from `node:fs` (zero new runtime dependencies). In the `db` command case, `--query-file` is parsed via `getFlag`. Priority logic: if `--query-file` is provided, its contents are read with `readFileSync` (UTF-8) and used as the query; errors (file not found, permission denied) are caught and printed with a clear message before `process.exit(1)`. If `--query-file` is absent, `--query` and positional args are used as before. PowerShell 5.1 help examples are corrected to use double-quoted outer strings so that backtick escape sequences are processed. Data flow: `--query-file <path>` → `readFileSync(path, 'utf8')` → query string → existing DB execution path.
steps.title: S4 Implementation: --query-file and PS Help in inspect db

steps.description: The APL confirms all four features meet ticket requirements. The code review verified: (1) run display uses correct field names `startedAt`/`finishedAt` with nullable type for QUEUED runs; (2) `--dry-run` prints payload and exits before any API call; (3) `--search` correctly passes a server-side query param; (4) `--query-file` reads SQL from a file with proper error handling and corrected PowerShell double-quoted help examples. No regressions: all existing command invocations are unchanged, no new runtime dependencies are introduced, and the codebase passes typecheck, build, and tests (S5 quality gates). The two fixes caught in review (nullable `startedAt` type and PS quoting) are low-risk and improve long-term maintainability.
steps.title: Summary: All Requirements Met, Two Correctness Fixes Applied
